title: SieveError struct literal repeated 9x without constructor
description: lib.rs constructs SieveError { message: format!(...), kind: ..., source: None } as a literal in nine separate call sites. Each repetition must remember to set source: None, which is error-prone. Idiomatic Rust would add a SieveError::new(kind: SieveErrorKind, message: String) -> Self constructor so call sites write SieveError::new(kind, format!(...)) instead. The pub(crate) source field is then encapsulated. This also removes the risk of accidentally omitting source: None in future additions.
